metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_325
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Using a ADCY8 knockout, it is shown that adenylyl cyclase 8 (AC8) is required for mossy fiber LTP.

-AC8 knockout displays a reduction in cAMP production (figure 1).
-Mossy fiber LTP is strongly reduced in the AC8 knockout (figure 4).

Numerous studies have demonstrated that mossy fiber LTP is expressed presynaptically (Zalutsky & Nicoll), and requires cAMP production and the activation of PKA. Since this form of LTP does not induce changes in presynaptic calcium influx (Kamiya et.al 2002), it must operate through potentiation of synaptic vesicle exocytosis.

In addition, it is shown that other forms of plasticity (PTP and PPF) are also affected by AC8 knockout (figure 4). This is in line with a defect in regulation of synaptic vesicle exocytosis. However, based on the available data, it cannot be excluded that other processes (e.g. potentiation of calcium influx) contribute to PTP or PPF at the mossy fiber synapse.. Experimental description: Mice lacking a functional AC8 gene were generated by deletion of exon 1 and 2 kb 5' flanking region through homologous recombination (Schaefer et al., 2000).
